title: andryala integrifolia var. nigricans
intro: Andryala integrifolia var. nigricans, also known as Black Andryala, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Andryala integrifolia plant, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils in its leaves and flowers. The medicinal properties of Black Andryala are said to provide health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture or infusion for internal use, and can also be used topically as a salve or ointment for skin conditions.
